reconciliation_note: Step-0 reconciliation: threed/racketsport/shot_taxonomy.py is the current shots producer via shot_taxonomy.classify_shots_from_payloads and ARTIFACT_TYPE racketsport_shots. That producer procedurally classifies SHOT_TYPES smash/lob/dink/drop/drive/atp/erne/tweener and carries serve/return/third-shot status separately in rally_index; scripts/racketsport/classify_shots.py writes shots.json from that producer. threed/racketsport/shot_classifier.py and the shot_trainable_baseline.py/shot_transfer_baseline.py siblings are scaffold, trainable, or transfer baselines rather than a declarative v0 rule table. rally_metrics.py is the coaching_facts producer and establishes the trust vocabulary ok/estimated/unverified_cue. shot_rules_v0 overlaps the existing vocabulary on smash/lob/dink/drop/drive and promotes serve/return from rally-index concepts into v0 class outputs; volley is new; atp/erne/tweener are out of scope for v0, as are fh/bh learned-baseline labels. Intended future integration: shot_taxonomy.classify_shots_from_payloads should consume this table while continuing to emit the existing racketsport_shots artifact, not create a second parallel racketsport_shots producer. threed/racketsport/shot_rules.py emits no artifact_type and only classifies in-memory feature dicts.

rules.rationale: runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md lines 2318-2331 define the S1 feature vector and line 2342 fixes serve as the first rally shot from the baseline; pickleball serves must start behind the baseline, so this arc-free rule gets the highest v0 seed.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md line 2343 fixes return as rally shot 2 after a bounce; pickleball returns are groundstrokes after the serve bounces, so this remains arc-free and high confidence.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md line 2344 fixes a >=3.0 m apex with baseline/transition landing; pickleball lobs are high arcs intended to push opponents back, so deep landing disambiguates them from soft kitchen shots.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md lines 2345-2346 fix high contact, >=12 m/s horizontal speed, and downward net crossing; pickleball smashes are fast overhead/downward attacks, so all three cues are required.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md line 2347 fixes volley as no previous bounce with kitchen/transition contact; pickleball volleys are air contacts near the non-volley/transition area, but v0 keeps confidence lower because arc cues are not required.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md lines 2348-2349 fix bounced kitchen contact, apex <1.2 m, and kitchen landing; pickleball dinks are low, soft kitchen-to-kitchen ground contacts, so this uses both contact and landing zones.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md lines 2350-2352 fix third-shot or baseline contact, kitchen landing, and apex <2.5 m; pickleball drops are soft attempts to land in the kitchen, especially the flagship third-shot-drop case.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md lines 2353-2354 fix drive as the fallback for fast, flatter remaining shots; pickleball drives are pace-first low-arc attacks, so this rule intentionally comes after the more specific classes.

runs/archive/root_docs_20260709/TECH_BLUEPRINTS.md line 2355 fixes unknown as the fallback, with lines 2356-2359 requiring null-feature rules to fail closed; this preserves honesty when the v0 feature evidence is insufficient.
